parentQuestion: Striking the "funny bone" (ulnar nerve) may cause injury to a nerve of which plexus?

scaffolds.q: The arm is supplied by a network of nerves called the brachial plexus.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The brachial plexus is the nerve network that supplies your entire arm - from shoulder to fingertips. 'Brachial' means relating to the arm.

scaffolds.q: 'Plexus' means a network or braid of nerves.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Plexus comes from Latin meaning 'braid.' Spinal nerves weave together into plexuses before distributing to specific body regions.

scaffolds.q: The brachial plexus is formed from spinal nerves in the ___ region.
scaffolds.explain: The neck region! The brachial plexus comes from C5-T1 (lower cervical and upper thoracic nerves). These nerves exit the neck and travel to supply the arm.

scaffolds.q: All the major nerves of the arm (median, ulnar, radial) come from the brachial plexus.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The brachial plexus gives rise to all the major arm nerves: median, ulnar, radial, musculocutaneous, and axillary. It's the master distribution center for the upper limb.

scaffolds.q: The ulnar nerve supplies sensation to the little finger and half the ring finger.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The ulnar nerve's sensory territory is the little finger and medial half of the ring finger. That's why hitting your 'funny bone' causes tingling in those specific fingers.

scaffolds.q: The ulnar nerve comes from C8 and T1 spinal nerve roots.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The ulnar nerve arises from C8-T1, the lowest roots of the brachial plexus. It comes from the medial cord of the plexus.

scaffolds.q: If the brachial plexus is damaged, arm function is affected.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Since the brachial plexus supplies the entire arm, damage here can cause widespread loss of movement and sensation. Severe injuries cause complete arm paralysis.

scaffolds.q: The ulnar nerve is a terminal branch of the brachial plexus, specifically from the medial cord.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The brachial plexus has three cords (lateral, medial, posterior). The ulnar nerve comes from the medial cord, which is formed from C8-T1 fibers.

scaffolds.q: Since the ulnar nerve comes from the brachial plexus, injury to this nerve is injury to a brachial plexus branch.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The ulnar nerve is a brachial plexus nerve. Any injury to the ulnar nerve (like hitting your funny bone) affects a nerve from the brachial plexus distribution.
